frame_analyses.description: Instruments positioned around Penrose drain with needle and suture visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup appears proper with drain in position

frame_analyses.description: Needle being guided through drain tissue near marking
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled needle passage through first mark

frame_analyses.description: Suture being pulled through drain, completing placement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good suture positioning through both marks

frame_analyses.description: Beginning formation of first throw with instruments crossing

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper double throw technique for surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Beginning second throw with hand switching apparent
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear hand switching from first to second throw

frame_analyses.description: Forming third and final throw with opposite hand direction
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching maintained for third throw
Both suture ends being cut inside trainer

frame_analyses.description: Task completed with cut suture ends and secured knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final result shows secure knot with proper closure

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw formation
Single throw with proper hand switching
Final single throw with hand switch, knot appears secure

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well closed with no visible gaps

technique_summary: Good execution of intracorporeal suturing with proper surgeon's knot formation and correct hand switching between throws. Suture placement accuracy was reasonable with minimal deviation.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise needle placement at the marked points
Consider slightly faster knot tying to improve overall time
strengths: Good knot security and drain closure
